title: cuscuta epithymum var. godronii
intro: Cuscuta epithymum var. godronii, also known as the dodder, is a type of parasitic flowering plant that grows on other plants, using them for nutrients and water. Traditionally, the dodder has been used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta epithymum var. godronii include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal preparations.
